note: S-095 HLP productization. apply_evalplan_fill gains a phase selector so the driver-free clean path (fill + core finalize) is reachable through the MCP surface in one call. The live contract_hash() has moved to PENDING_CONTRACT_HASH; RELEASED_CONTRACT_HASH stays frozen at the 5.0.0 receipt until the release train collapses the two. Not yet released.

addedInputParameters.name: phase
addedInputParameters.description: How far the recipe runs. 'structural' = confident deletions only; 'all' (default; the prior behaviour) = structural + per-region content fills; 'clean' = 'all' plus core's deterministic post-fill cleanup (title/teacher/정의적 fills, instruction-scaffolding + orphan prune, red-strip, blue->black recolor, caption strip), surfaced under contentReport.finalize. An invalid phase fails closed with EVALPLAN_PHASE_INVALID.

coreDependency.failClosed: phase not in {structural, all, clean} raises EVALPLAN_PHASE_INVALID before any core call. phase='clean' is inert against a core build whose fill_evalplan lacks the clean branch (it degrades to 'all'); MIN_PYTHON_HWPX stays 4.0.0 in this branch. The released constant only flips at the release train.

proof: Holding version coordinates, classification, counts, domains, and every other tool fixed, the canonical payload delta is confined to apply_evalplan_fill: its inputSchema gains one optional string property (phase, default 'all'), which is not added to the required list. No tool is added, removed, renamed, or reclassified; the output schema is unchanged (contentReport is a free-form object that already carries the new finalize report); mutation/profile/skill-required flags are unchanged. Contract hash c2cd81fdb3089bae -> 41a685020447bd8b (recorded as PENDING_CONTRACT_HASH until release).
